jobs.title: Associate Director, IT Service Delivery
jobs.job_description: Scholar Rock is a biopharmaceutical company that discovers, develops, and delivers life-changing therapies for people with serious diseases that have high unmet need. As a global leader in the biology of the transforming growth factor beta (TGFβ) superfamily of cell proteins and named for the visual resemblance of a scholar rock to protein structures, the clinical-stage company is focused on advancing innovative treatments where protein growth factors are fundamental. Over the past decade, the company has created a pipeline with the potential to advance the standard of care for neuromuscular disease, cardiometabolic disorders, cancer, and other conditions where growth factor-targeted drugs can play a transformational role. Scholar Rock is the only company to show clinical proof of concept for a muscle-targeted treatment in spinal muscular atrophy (SMA). This commitment to unlocking fundamentally different therapeutic approaches is powered by broad application of a proprietary platform, which has developed novel monoclonal antibodies to modulate protein growth factors with extraordinary selectivity. By harnessing cutting-edge science in disease spaces that are historically under-addressed through traditional therapies, Scholar Rock works every day to create new possibilities for patients. Learn more about the company’s approach at ScholarRock.com and follow @ScholarRock and on LinkedIn. Summary of Position: Scholar Rock is transitioning from a clinical-stage organization to a commercial-stage biotech, and IT Service Delivery sits at the center of that transformation. We are looking for a dynamic, hands-on leader to stabilize and elevate our IT service desk while laying the foundation for a modern, scalable, and customer-centric service model. This role offers significant visibility, ownership, and influence. You will shape how IT is experienced across the company—supporting a diverse workforce of onsite, hybrid, and remote users—while partnering closely with internal teams and external managed service providers. As Associate Director/ Director of IT Service Delivery, you will: • Own the end-to-end employee IT support experience • Lead internal support staff and manage external managed service providers • Establish operational rigor, service reliability, and stakeholder trust • Drive continuous improvement and long-term transformation of IT service delivery You will stabilize what exists today, then evolve it into a best-in-class service organization aligned with a regulated, fast-growing biotech environment.

jobs.title: Multimodal AI PhD Intern (Spring 2026)
jobs.job_description: Who we are. Reality Defender is an award-winning cybersecurity company helping enterprises and governments detect deepfakes and AI-generated media. Utilizing a patented multi-model approach, Reality Defender is robust against the bleeding edge of generative platforms producing video, audio, imagery, and text media. Reality Defender's API-first deepfake detection platform empowers teams and developers alike to identify fraud, disinformation campaigns, and harmful deepfakes in real time. Backed by world class investors including DCVC, Illuminate Financial, Y Combinator, Booz Allen Hamilton, IBM, Accenture, Rackhouse, and Argon VC, Reality Defender works with leading enterprise clients, financial institutions, and governments in order to ensure AI-generated media is not used for malicious purposes. Youtube: Reality Defender Wins RSA Most Innovative Startup The Multimodal AI Internship. The 4-month internship is designed for current PhD students and candidates to partner with Reality Defender's AI team to conduct cutting-edge research and publish peer-reviewed papers. Your primary collaborators will be Surya Koppisetti and Yi Zhu , who will guide and advise your efforts within multi-modal deepfake detection. This internship can be performed remotely, although you're welcome to work from our HQ in New York City. What you'll do. Investigate and propose new methods for detecting generative multi-modal content, spanning audio and vision modalities. Perform research on multi-modal deepfake detection and reasoning tasks. Collaborate with researchers in the team. Write up results of research for internal reports and submission to academic journals/workshops. Independently implement and evaluate ideas on modern deep learning stack - Python, PyTorch, and GPU-enabled cloud compute, like AWS/GCP. Who you are. PhD student in a relevant technical field, preferably three or more years into the program Experience in multi-modal learning, such as in audio-visual classification and audio-language reasoning. Proficient in Python and in building deep learning models with PyTorch. Published peer-reviewed research papers in reputable AI and speech venues, e.g. CVPR, NeurIPS, ACL, Interspeech. Excited about Reality Defender's mission to build a best-in-class and comprehensive deepfake and AI-generated content detection platform. Available to start in Spring 2026, for a minimum duration of 4 months.

jobs.title: Improv Actor - AI Trainer
jobs.job_description: Opportunity Overview Handshake AI is inviting actors, improvisers, and performers to join a paid, collaborative improv project to work with one of the leading AI companies. You'll be matched with other performers over video and given a light prompt or scenario to explore together. Sessions are unscripted and open-ended. You'll improvise scenes, explore characters, and respond naturally in the moment, with plenty of creative freedom to shape how each interaction unfolds. Projects are remote, part-time, and flexible, making them easy to fit alongside auditions, classes, or rehearsals. Project Details Improvise scenes and dialogue using light prompts, personality notes, and creative constraints. You'll have wide latitude to explore tone, emotion, and character choices. Collaborate with other performers by building on scenarios and interactions that feel grounded, human, and fun to play. Experiment with character, emotion, and subtext, drawing on your acting instincts to create natural, engaging moments. Qualifications Acting or Improv Experience: Background in improv, acting, theater, sketch, or other performance-based work (formal training or real-world experience both welcome). Strong Creative Instincts: You enjoy making bold choices, playing with character, and following the moment where it leads. Emotional Awareness: Ability to recognize, express, and shift between emotions in a way that feels authentic and human. Consistency & Care: You can stay true to a character's voice and emotional logic across a scene or interaction. Selection for each project is limited to a small cohort chosen for their disciplinary and research expertise. We are seeking experts in new research areas to test the limits of the world’s top LLMs’ understanding by completing tasks that positively impact the next frontier AI model. Application Process Create a Handshake account Upload your resume and verify your identity Get matched and onboarded into relevant projects Start working and earning Work authorization information F-1 students who are eligible for CPT or OPT may be eligible for projects on Handshake AI. Work with your Designated School Official to determine your eligibility. If your school requires a CPT course, Handshake AI may not meet your school’s requirements. STEM OPT is not supported. for more information on what types of work authorizations are supported on Handshake AI.
